howTo.description: Step-by-step guide to converting PNG files to WebP.

steps.name: Add PNG files
steps.text: Drag PNG files onto the drop zone or click to browse. Multiple files supported.

steps.name: Set WebP quality
steps.text: Adjust the quality slider (60–95%). Default 80% balances size and quality.

steps.name: Download WebP files
steps.text: Download files individually or all at once. File sizes shown for comparison.

contentBlocks.title: Why convert PNG to WebP?
contentBlocks.html:
PNG files are lossless and support transparency, but they can be very large. WebP offers both lossy and lossless compression with transparency support — resulting in files that are significantly smaller than PNG.

For web use, WebP is the ideal replacement for PNG. Modern browsers universally support it, and the size savings directly improve page speed and Core Web Vitals.

This tool requires a desktop browser for WebP encoding. All processing happens locally.

contentBlocks.title: How to convert PNG to WebP

items.title: 1. Add PNG files
items.description: Drag your PNG images onto the upload area or click to select them. No limit on files.

items.title: 2. Set quality
items.description: Choose the WebP quality level. Lower value = smaller file, higher = sharper image.

items.title: 3. Download WebP
items.description: Download each file or grab them all at once. Size comparison shown for every file.

contentBlocks.title: When should you convert PNG to WebP?

items.title: Website assets
items.description: Replace PNG logos, icons, and UI graphics with WebP to reduce page weight without losing transparency.

items.title: Transparent graphics
items.description: Unlike JPG, WebP preserves alpha transparency. Perfect for logos on coloured backgrounds.

items.title: Page speed
items.description: Smaller images improve load times and Core Web Vitals scores.

items.title: Storage savings
items.description: WebP files are typically 30–50% smaller than PNG, saving bandwidth and storage.

contentBlocks.title: What makes this PNG to WebP converter different?

items.title: Complete privacy
items.description: Files never leave your computer. Conversion uses the browser Canvas API — nothing is uploaded.

items.title: No file limit
items.description: Convert as many images as you need. All files processed in sequence.

items.title: Instant processing
items.description: Conversion happens natively in your browser. Results are ready in seconds.

items.title: One-click download

contentBlocks.title: Frequently asked questions about PNG to WebP conversion

items.question: Is transparency preserved?
items.answer: Yes. WebP supports alpha-channel transparency just like PNG.

items.question: How much smaller will the WebP be?
items.answer: Typically 30–50% smaller than PNG. Exact savings depend on image content.

items.question: Is quality lost?
items.answer: WebP lossy mode discards some detail, but at 80% quality the difference is negligible.

items.question: Do all browsers support WebP?
items.answer: Yes. All major modern browsers support WebP.

items.question: Why is this desktop-only?
items.answer: WebP encoding via Canvas API requires desktop browser support.

items.question: Are files uploaded to a server?
items.answer: No. Everything runs locally in your browser.

items.question: What is the maximum file size?
items.answer: Individual files up to 100 MB are supported.
